title: Production Auth / Secrets / Config Hardening
context: GL-200A, GL-200B, and GL-200C are merged internally. GL-201 is the first dedicated hardening pass for production auth, secret handling, and runtime configuration safety following the GL-200 tenant/workspace block. GrantLayer remains Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries.
scope: Focused production auth/secrets/config hardening only. Changes to config.py (placeholder/weak token detection, CORS localhost warning), GL-201 test suite, and documentation artifacts.
non_goals: Public website work or public publish
current_state_summary: Prior to GL-201, startup_errors() checked for missing REQUIRE_ADMIN_TOKEN, missing GRANTLAYER_ADMIN_TOKEN, missing REQUIRE_CHALLENGE, and demo endpoints enabled. Gaps: no rejection of placeholder/demo/weak admin tokens in production-like modes; no minimum token length enforcement; no bootstrap operator token validation; no CORS localhost warning in production-like mode. Auth, operator, and secret handling were already sound (hmac.compare_digest, PBKDF2, no raw token exposure).

config_py_changes: Added _UNSAFE_PLACEHOLDER_TOKENS frozenset of known unsafe placeholder values
Added _PROD_MIN_ADMIN_TOKEN_LENGTH = 16 minimum character length for production
startup_errors() now rejects placeholder/short admin tokens in production-like modes
startup_errors() now rejects placeholder bootstrap operator tokens in production-like modes
startup_warnings() now warns when CORS origins include localhost in production-like mode
Raw token values are never included in error or warning messages
verified_no_changes_needed: Operator token uses PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with 600,000 iterations
Operator to_dict() never exposes token_hash or raw token
Auth failure responses never echo attempted token values

operator_token_safety.lookup_hash: SHA-256 for O(1) narrowing before PBKDF2

compatibility_notes: GL-190 demo endpoint safety tests use short tokens in local mode — GL-201 placeholder check is production-like mode only, no regression
GL-089 startup_ok/startup_errors tests remain compatible — placeholder check is mode-gated
startup_warnings() CORS localhost warning is informational only, not fatal, no existing test broken
remaining_gaps: Secret rotation/expiry automation — deferred to future GL
External secret manager integration — deferred to future GL
Argon2id upgrade for operator token hashing — deferred to future GL
Full secondary-path tenant isolation for evidence/provenance — deferred to future GL
workspace_id enforcement (currently nullable/reserved) — deferred to future GL
Database-level secret encryption at rest — GL-202+
production_readiness_impact: GL-201 is a hardening step, not a production SaaS readiness declaration. Production-like mode startup now explicitly rejects placeholder and weak admin tokens. GrantLayer remains Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries.
decision_rationale: All GL-201 objectives met: production fail-closed hardened with placeholder/weak token rejection; admin and operator token behavior verified safe; demo/dev/test flag boundary verified; CORS reviewed with localhost warning added; secret leakage tests added; GL-200 tenant/workspace regression verified clean.
recommended_next_issues: GL-201 Merge — merge internal branch to main after validation
GL-202 Persistence / Postgres / Migration Readiness — after GL-201 is merged
